name: Prosciutto Di Parma with Warm Fig Tartlets
recipeIngredient: 12 tablespoons sweet unsalted butter, cut into pieces
8 ounces Prosciutto di Parma, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Butter a baking dish and set aside.
3. On a floured surface, make a mound of flour and create a well in the center. Place sugar, eggs, and melted butter into the well. Using a fork, mix the ingredients until a rough dough forms.
4. Bring the dough together and knead for one minute to form a medium ball. Divide the dough into four pieces and shape each into balls. Refrigerate for 15 minutes to allow them to rest.
5. Meanwhile, cut the figs in half.
6. Remove the pastry from the refrigerator and roll out each piece into a 4- to 5-inch circle, about 1/4 inch thick. Arrange the cut fruit, cut side up, onto each round.
7. Bake the tarts at 400°F for 20 to 22 minutes.
8. Remove the tarts from the oven and top with two thin slices of Prosciutto. Sprinkle with fennel seeds and extra virgin olive oil. Serve.
Yield: 4 servings
